## CONTENT_BLOCK

### Summary
Answers to common questions about water bills, meter reading, and payments.

### Questions and Answers
- Q: How is my water bill calculated?
  A: Your bill = (volume used × tariff per kilolitre) + fixed service fee + sewerage charge (80% of water used). Tariffs are tiered: first 5 kL at lower rate, above at higher rate.

- Q: How do I read my water meter?
  A: The meter is usually in a concrete box near the street. Read the black numbers (kilolitres). Subtract last month’s reading to get your usage. Send a photo to water.meter@harare.gov.zw if you suspect a mistake.

- Q: I think my bill is too high. What can I do?
  A: First check for leaks (turn off all taps, see if meter still moves). Then request a re‑reading by calling 0800 1234. If still unresolved, visit the water billing office at Selous Avenue.

- Q: Can I pay my bill online?
  A: Yes. Use the council portal (pay.harare.gov.zw) or mobile money (EcoCash, OneMoney) using biller code 2020. Also possible via CABS and NMB bank apps.

- Q: What happens if I don’t pay?
  A: After 30 days, a 10% penalty is added. After 90 days, water may be disconnected. Reconnection fee is 400 ZiG plus full settlement of arrears.

- Q: How do I report a billing error?
  A: Send an email to water.billing@harare.gov.zw with your account number and a photo of your meter. Response within 5 working days.
